content.canonical_text: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol that enables AI applications to securely access tools and data from external sources. MCP uses a client-server architecture where AI applications (clients) connect to servers that provide resources, tools, and prompts. Capability discovery in MCP works through a negotiation mechanism: during initialization, the client sends its capabilities and the server responds with its supported capabilities. Tools are discovered via tools/list and called via tools/call. Resources use a URI-based addressing scheme with resource templates for parameterized access. Prompts are pre-written templates that guide AI behavior. MCP supports transports including stdio (for local processes) and SSE (for remote servers). The protocol is designed to be transport-agnostic and supports streaming responses for long-running operations.
